mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL > 3.0) signals central bank intervention risks, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in rate hikes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns drops below -0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates robust supply-side expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and PAYEMS shows a coefficient < 0.05 in 3-year rolling regressions, or 20d returns in co-expansionary periods fall below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 4%) with rising payroll growth (PAYEMS > 100k) signals strong wage pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as inflation fears dominate.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE < 4.0 and PAYEMS > 100k, or the interaction term's t-statistic < 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST > 1400) indicate resilient demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as construction activity fuels economic growth.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns in high HOUST periods fall below 0.3% or the correlation between HOUST and SPX returns drops below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Declining consumer spending (RSAFS < 690k) signals weak demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as corporate earnings face downward pressure.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS < 690k, or the coefficient on RSAFS in 3-year regressions is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Weak industrial production (INDPRO < 100) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3.0) indicates stagflation risks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL shows a coefficient > 0.05 in 3-year rolling regressions, or 20d returns in stagflationary periods exceed -0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1 > 2.5%) signals robust demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate profits expand.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns in high GDPC1 periods fall below 0.5% or the correlation between GDPC1 and SPX returns drops below 0.2.
